sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Melissa Sanchez, is a 59-year-old female who is being seen for a follow-up appointment for status post mitral valve repair done on 8/3/2020.
She reports feeling "pretty good" and "definitely feeling better" since the surgery, with some residual chest discomfort and shallow breathing.
She experiences chest pains and shallow breathing, but notes that these episodes are intermittent and last only a few minutes.
She has been learning to manage these symptoms by slowing down when she feels them and taking medication as prescribed.
The patient has a history of mitral regurgitation, atrial fibrillation, diabetes, and asthma, and has had negative cardiac workup for coronary artery disease.
She is currently taking coumadin 4mg, lasix 4mg/day, and atenolol 50mg/day.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of mitral regurgitation, atrial fibrillation, diabetes, and asthma, and has had negative cardiac workup for coronary artery disease.
Review of systems: The patient reports intermittent chest pains and shallow breathing, but notes that these symptoms are not constant and are manageable with lifestyle changes.
Current medications: Coumadin 4mg, Lasix 4mg/day, Atenolol 50mg/day.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's head and neck examination revealed no jugular venous distension (JVD).
The lungs have reduced breath sounds, but auscultation and percussion are clear.
The patient's heart rhythm is irregularly irregular, which is consistent with atrial fibrillation.
The S1 is slightly accentuated, and there is no S3.
The patient reports no abdominal pain and no pain upon palpation of the extremities.
There is trace peripheral edema on the extremities.
Test Results: The patient's ECG taken on 12/26/2020 reveals atrial fibrillation with a controlled ventricular response and T-wave inversion anteriorly.
Assessment / Problem List:

Assessment: The patient is a 59-year-old female with a history of mitral regurgitation, atrial fibrillation, diabetes, and asthma, who is recovering well from mitral valve repair surgery and is being managed with coumadin, lasix, and atenolol.
Problem list: Atrial fibrillation; Mitral regurgitation; Diabetes; Asthma
Plan:
I will continue the patient's current medications, including coumadin 4mg, lasix 4mg/day, and atenolol 50mg/day.
I will advise the patient to continue to manage her symptoms with lifestyle changes and to remain active.
Follow-up: I will schedule a follow-up appointment in 6-9 months to check on the patient's progress.
